assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 for a foreign exchange exposure arising from an intragroup dividend-related receivable.
The issue is limited to whether the exposure could be designated within an IFRS hedge accounting model.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to changes in fair value of a recognised asset or liability attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows of a recognised asset or liability or a highly probable forecast transaction.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for qualifying foreign currency exposures.
